name: app.R
landingfile.R
sample_csv.R
content:
# ==============================================================================
# AUSTATS - PRO SAMPLE DATASET GENERATOR (WITH REALISTIC SIGNIFICANCE)
# ==============================================================================

generate_sample_data <- function() {
  set.seed(123) # Semilla fija para reproducibilidad total
  n_total <- 1500
  
  # 1. VARIABLE DE AGRUPACIÓN (Desbalanceada)
  Group <- c(rep("Treatment", 650), rep("Control", 850))
  
  # 2. CONTINUA NO NORMAL SIGNIFICATIVA (Mann-Whitney U Test -> p < 0.001)
  Age_Treat <- round(rgamma(650, shape = 4, rate = 0.1) + 15)   # Media ~ 55
  Age_Control <- round(rgamma(850, shape = 6, rate = 0.1) + 15) # Media ~ 75
  Age <- c(Age_Treat, Age_Control)
  
  # 3. CONTINUA NORMAL NO SIGNIFICATIVA (Student's t-test -> Realista ~ 0.20)
  # Aplicamos cuantiles teóricos para engañar a Shapiro, pero damos
  # una diferencia leve (1.5 mmHg) entre grupos para que no dé p=1.000
  perfect_norm_treat <- sample(qnorm(ppoints(650))) * 12 + 120.0
  perfect_norm_control <- sample(qnorm(ppoints(850))) * 12 + 121.5
  Systolic_BP <- round(c(perfect_norm_treat, perfect_norm_control), 1)
  
  # 4. CATEGÓRICA NO SIGNIFICATIVA (Chi-squared -> Realista ~ 0.15)
  # Leves diferencias en probabilidad (50/50 vs 54/46) para que el p-valor sea natural.
  Gender_Treat <- sample(c("Male", "Female"), 650, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.50, 0.50))
  Gender_Control <- sample(c("Male", "Female"), 850, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.54, 0.46))
  Gender <- c(Gender_Treat, Gender_Control)
  # Añadimos ~45 NAs (3% missing) al azar para la alerta amarilla.
  Gender[sample(1:n_total, 45)] <- NA 
  
  # 5. CATEGÓRICA SIGNIFICATIVA (Chi-squared -> p < 0.001)
  Smoke_Treat <- sample(c("Never", "Former", "Current"), 650, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.7, 0.2, 0.1))
  Smoke_Control <- sample(c("Never", "Former", "Current"), 850, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.4, 0.3, 0.3))
  Smoking_Status <- c(Smoke_Treat, Smoke_Control)
  
  # 6. CONTINUA CON ALTA PÉRDIDA (Alerta Roja > 10% Missing)
  Quality_of_Life <- round(rnorm(n_total, mean = 75, sd = 10))
  Quality_of_Life[Quality_of_Life > 100] <- 100 # Tope visual
  Quality_of_Life[sample(1:n_total, 200)] <- NA # 13.3% missing
  
  # 7. CATEGÓRICA RARA (Fisher's Exact Test)
  Rare_Complication <- rep("No", n_total)
  Rare_Complication[sample(1:650, 3)] <- "Yes"           
  Rare_Complication[650 + sample(1:850, 1)] <- "Yes"     
  
  # 8. Ensamblamos el Dataframe (Respetando el orden de Treatment y Control)
  df_demo <- data.frame(
    Group, Age, Gender, Systolic_BP, Quality_of_Life, 
    Smoking_Status, Rare_Complication, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  # 9. Shuffling (Desordenar filas)
  # Mezclamos todo para simular un Excel real.
  df_demo <- df_demo[sample(nrow(df_demo)), ]
  rownames(df_demo) <- NULL 
  
  return(df_demo)
}
